claim: The basis trade ($800B-$1T at 50-100x leverage) has structurally altered the Treasury-to-credit contagion channel, impairing the flight-to-quality assumption
needs: Independent corroboration from multiple agents, updated size and leverage estimates post-2020 regulatory response, quantification of degree of flight-to-quality impairment

claim: Credit-equity lead-lag has compressed from 6-8 months to 0-2 months post-2015, potentially eliminating the most actionable cross-asset early warning signal
needs: More than 5 independent turning points post-2015 to confirm structural rather than low-vol regime artifact; out-of-sample validation across regimes

claim: Covenant-lite structures reduce aggregate default rates not just delay them, with 2.2% cumulative vs 3.8% for covenant-heavy through COVID
needs: Observation of a full credit cycle without unprecedented policy support; testing under organic deterioration with higher absolute funding costs; longer time series beyond one cycle plus COVID

claim: Sanctions architecture functions as a shadow credit rating system with embedded credit options, validated by Russia 2022
needs: Independent corroboration, rigorous methodology for estimating sanctions probability for China/Taiwan, framework for sanctions-adjusted recovery rate modeling

claim: Strategic zombies sustained by national security rationale represent a new class of firms with distinct credit dynamics synthesizing Japan and geopolitical mechanisms
needs: Independent corroboration, quantification of total scale of geopolitically-subsidized firms, assessment of subsidy durability through fiscal consolidation

claim: Gold miner credit is in a structurally favorable position due to central bank buying regime shift of 1,000+ tonnes per year post-2022
needs: Independent corroboration, analysis of whether central bank buying is front-loaded diversification that plateaus, credit metric sensitivity at various gold price scenarios

claim: Currency-hedged non-US credit offers structural 150-300bp pickup versus US IG
needs: Analysis of persistence under convergent central bank policies, identification of regulatory and institutional arbitrage barriers, hedging cost volatility assessment

claim: Futures curve structure (contango vs backwardation) is a first-order credit input for commodity producers that is genuinely underappreciated
needs: Backtested relationship between curve structure changes and commodity issuer spread moves, forward-looking utility assessment given curve unpredictability

claim: EM corporate credit is bifurcating along a dollar-exposure axis with India/ASEAN structurally improving while frontier markets remain vulnerable
needs: Stress testing of bifurcation under extreme dollar strengthening, analysis of whether taper tantrum and 2018 EM episodes represent informative partial tests

claim: Twin deficit trajectory requiring $2.5-3T annual foreign capital inflow may become a binding constraint on foreign demand for US corporate credit
needs: Identification of nonlinear threshold at which twin deficits become binding with no clean historical analog, decomposition of marginal corporate bond buyer nationality and Treasury crowding-out sensitivity
